longDescription:
Keep a table of leads or customers, each with a line of context, and this turns every row into its own piece of media. On a schedule it reads the table and, per row, generates a personalized marketing image or a short clip, saves it, and emails that person a link to their own asset.

It forks on the medium you choose. For images it makes them all at once. For video it works one row at a time: each clip is an async job, and the run pauses until a webhook says that clip is ready before starting the next — so a long batch survives the wait instead of holding a connection open. The prompt for each row is built from that row's context, so nothing is generic.

The first run seeds a small demo table so it works immediately; point `dbHandle` at your own table when you're ready. Set `dryRun` and it reads the rows and shows the prompt each would get, generating and sending nothing.

You pay for the media it generates — one image, or one clip, per row. Video costs more than images, so keep `limit` small while you try it. The emails and the table are effectively free.
useCases: Send every lead in a table a marketing image built from their own interests, on a daily cadence.
Generate a short personalized clip per customer for a launch or a win-back campaign.
Preview the per-row prompts with dryRun before spending anything on generation.
notes:
Click **Use this template** — Sapiom builds and deploys it for you, then run it from the workflow page. Your $5 signup credit covers first runs.

It runs on a seeded demo table out of the box, so a first run needs no input. `medium` picks `image` (default, cheaper, made all at once) or `video` (async, one row at a time, pricier). `limit` caps how many rows you render per run (default 3, max 25) — keep it small for video. Set `dryRun: true` to read the rows and the prompt each would get without generating or emailing anything. Point `dbHandle` at your own table (columns: `name`, `email`, `context`) once you're past the demo. On a live run it emails each row's address, so use `dryRun` or a test table first.

To run it on a cadence, attach the `schedule` (default `0 9 * * *`) as a cron trigger on the deployed agent.

Prefer to work from the code? Run it locally with `run_local` and `{ "dryRun": true }` to trace the whole graph for free, or edit and deploy it with the Sapiom MCP.

examples.title: Plan only (dryRun — no billed generation, no email)

planned.name: Ada Lovelace
planned.prompt: A personalized marketing image for Ada Lovelace. Theme: loves vintage computing and long-distance cycling. Style: warm, editorial. No text, no watermark.
